questions.question: What is the output sequence of balls (color and destination) for this puzzle?
questions.answer: Three blue balls reach the bottom, then two red balls reach the bottom, and the fourth red ball goes into the interceptor.

questions.question: How does this puzzle use two 2-bit counters differently from Challenge 16?
questions.answer: Challenge 16 uses one 2-bit counter to count balls and stop after 4. Challenge 17 uses two 2-bit counters - one for blue balls on the left side and one for red balls on the right side - creating independent counting mechanisms.

questions.question: Why does the 4th red ball go to the interceptor instead of the bottom?
questions.answer: The right-side 2-bit counter tracks how many red balls have been released. After 3 red balls reach the bottom (binary 11), the 4th ball flips the counter to trigger the interceptor path.
